headline: The recommended educators converge on rethinking how AI agents and coding tools reshape software work.
body: IBM Technology argues AI speeds coding but does not lift productivity unless the SDLC itself is redesigned around agents for testing and delivery [2], while Cyril Imhof contends agent building demands startup-style iteration over traditional engineering [4]. Julia Turc dissects realtime voice architecture through Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS, covering modular versus end-to-end design, audio tokenization via vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, and milestones from WaveNet to Whisper Streaming [1]. On the infrastructure side, Better Stack details Apple Container Machines, persistent Linux environments on Apple Silicon with systemd and multi-distro support [3], and Cyril Imhof notes Microsoft folding Nvidia chip technology into laptops for on-device AI acceleration [5]. Beyond the inner circle, Google for Developers demos Gemma running 10 parallel subagents under one orchestrator above 170 tokens per second [10], and Cursor's Ryo Lu warns against losing direct connection to code as AI turns software into a black box [8].
